name: ai-escalation-alert
title: AI Escalation Alert
description: Non-dismissible human-review surface with a stated reason, a severity rail, and a caller-owned escalation action.

props.name: title
props.description: Required. The condition that needs a human, stated plainly.

props.name: severity
props.description: Drives the rail, icon, and badge. Urgent pulses the rail under motion-safe.

props.name: children
props.description: Why this was raised, and by what service.

props.name: action
props.description: Caller-owned escalation action, e.g. a link into the local protocol.

props.name: dismissible
props.description: Off by default. An escalation that can be swiped away without acting is not an escalation.

clinicalNotes: This presents an escalation signal for a human to act on. It must never be described — in your copy or your product — as emergency triage, and it does not replace a local protocol: the action slot exists so it can point at one. Say what raised the flag ("flagged by your lab-rules service") rather than implying the model made a clinical judgement; a model-authored escalation with no named source teaches clinicians to distrust every future one. It renders role="alert" so assistive tech announces it immediately, and it is non-dismissible by default. If you do enable `dismissible`, log the dismissal — an unacted escalation that silently disappears is the failure mode this component exists to prevent.
notes: Severity is redundantly encoded: rail, icon, badge text, and tone — never colour alone.
